name: Speckknoedel (Austrian Bacon Dumplings)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the bread slices and cut into 1/2 inch cubes.
2. Cut bacon slices into 1/3 inch squares. Saute them over moderate heat in a large skillet for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently. Transfer to paper towels with a slotted spoon and pat dry.
3. Pour water to a depth of 3 inches into a wide bottomed pot and bring to simmer (for step 8).
4. Brown bread cubes in hot bacon fat for 3 to 5 minutes. Transfer to bowl.
5. Add cream to bowl. Gently toss bread until fully absorbed. Add bacon, flour, baking powder, caraway seeds, thyme, pepper, and 1/4 tsp salt. Beat egg yolk and add to bowl. Blend all ingredients.
6. Shape mixture into 1 1/4 inch spheres with hands (moisten slightly dry mix with more cream if needed). Place dumplings on plate in one layer without touching.
7. Melt butter to moderate heat in clean large skillet. Add onions and saute for 2 minutes. Add sauerkraut and remaining salt, stirring. Cover and cook for 12 minutes.
8. Cook dumplings in simmering water for about 10 minutes (start when onion-sauerkraut pan is covered). Do not turn dumplings as they will move by themselves.
9. Transfer cooked speckknoedel to warm bowl and cover with onion-sauerkraut mixture. Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.
Serves 3 to 4.
